summary: Joel Spolsky briefly reviews Tom DeMarco's essay collection 'Why Does Software Cost So Much?', recommending it based on DeMarco's credentials as co-author of Peopleware. He considers Peopleware the best book ever written about software management and praises DeMarco as a genius of software project management.
key_points: Peopleware is endorsed as the all-time best book on software management
Tom DeMarco is praised as the world's leading genius of software project management
About half the essays in the collection are highlighted as proving DeMarco's expertise
money_quotes: Peopleware, which is the all-time best book about software management ever written.
About half the essays prove that Tom DeMarco is simply the world's leading genious of software project management, and you shouldn't skip this book.
key_insight: Tom DeMarco's writings, especially Peopleware, represent essential reading for anyone involved in software project management.
